text:
Are you a fan of the #Python programming language? 🐍 Are you thrilled by the thought of how successful #Africa could be if given the right opportunities? 🚀 And do you have some spare ~500 CZK? 💰 I can recommend a perfect cause you can support with this money! 💸

In just a few days, my dearest PyCon Namibia takes place. You may not be able to attend this year, it's probably too late for that, but you can still support their cause! Individual ticket starts around ~500 CZK.

I've visited na.pycon.org in 2020, right before all the covid mayhem. It was a life-changing experience which I totally recommend if you're into exploring the world's PyCons. Not only for the touristic aspect of visiting #Namibia 🇳🇦 and its deserts, but mainly because at #PyConNA, I've met the truest people ❤️

I can assure you that the organizers of this conference, led by Ngazetungue Muheue and Jessica Upani, put the purest intentions in everything they do. With very little resources they're able to make insane impact in their community. I know them personally from both my trip to Namibia and their recent visit to #UbuntuSummit, Prague.

I bought a ticket for this year's na.pycon.org myself, and I told Muheue to give it to someone who needs it. It would make my day if I convinced at least one person by this status to buy a ticket too, so that they have a bit more resources to continue with their programming activities for students, getting computer labs for kids, and organizing Python events 🥲

Please, let me know in the comment, if you do buy a ticket to support them. It would really make me super happy!

profile.occupation: Software Engineer | Open Source Community Leader | Founder, Ubuntu Namibia | Co-founder, Python & Django Namibia | PyCon Namibia Organizer | Active in Python/Django Communities Worldwide

comments.text: I regularly support students (not only) visiting Czech conferences like WebExpo so why not support folks elsewhere, too... So, to whoever this helps to attend the event (and if I've done my math correctly, it should help 3 people): enjoy :-) Thanks for sharing the cause, Honza!

author.occupation: Security engineer & hacker, web developer, speaker and consultant

comments.text:
Thank you Honza and everyone else who helped.

Our community is expanding, and we make use of little we have to grow the community. The chilly conditions in Prague will always be in my memory. I have never experienced such weather in my life. It's hot in Namibia. 

To us, every donation is greatly appreciated. The conference's leftover funds are invested in other local Python activities, buying books, and students with computers and etc .

People in Czech are friendly and i love it.

author.occupation: Software Engineer | Open Source Community Leader | Founder, Ubuntu Namibia | Co-founder, Python & Django Namibia | PyCon Namibia Organizer | Active in Python/Django Communities Worldwide

comments.text: I hope it'll make its real holder happy 🙂

author.occupation: SEO/UX Specialist at Webmium ✅ Aspiring Backend Developer at Home ✅

comments.text:
Buy tickets here: https://na.pycon.org/tickets/

It's safe, I've put my own card there and bought my ticket using the form 🙂 Prices are in Namibian dollars, the charge is in South African rands. They're 1:1, so don't worry, it's the same.

author.occupation: Tvořím junior.guru, které pomáhá juniorním programátorům

comments.text: Read more about what they do and how they do it in an interview I made with Jessica Upani 2 years ago on my blog: https://honzajavorek.cz/blog/jessica-upani-about-python-events-in-namibia-you-have-to-be-pure-in-terms-of-your-why/
